title: Fixing Node.js Express Middleware for Route Parameter Validation

statement.markdown:
In this challenge, you are given a Node.js Express application with a middleware function that is supposed to validate the route parameter 'id'. The middleware function is implemented but contains a logical bug. Your task is to fix the bug and ensure that the middleware function correctly validates the route parameter 'id' before allowing the route to proceed.

The middleware function should return an HTTP error response (404) if the 'id' parameter is not a positive integer. Otherwise, it should allow the route to proceed.

You can assume that the route parameter 'id' will always be a string. You should write your middleware function to parse the 'id' string, check if it is a positive integer, and return an HTTP error response (404) if it is not.

Note: You can use the built-in 'parseInt' function to convert the 'id' string to an integer.

### Constraints

The input 'id' will always be a string and will be at least 1 character long.

### Examples

* Input: '/users/123'
Output: Allow the route to proceed
* Input: '/users/-123'
Output: Return an HTTP error response (404)
* Input: '/users/abc'
Output: Return an HTTP error response (404)
statement.outputFormat: Either allow the route to proceed or return an HTTP error response (404)
statement.notes:
You can use the built-in 'parseInt' function to convert the 'id' string to an integer.

### Constraints

The input 'id' will always be a string and will be at least 1 character long.

hints.text: Use the built-in 'parseInt' function to convert the 'id' string to an integer.

editorial.title: Fixing Node.js Express Middleware for Route Parameter Validation
editorial.intuition: The middleware function should return an HTTP error response (404) if the 'id' parameter is not a positive integer.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use the built-in 'parseInt' function to convert the 'id' string to an integer and then check if it is a positive integer.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regular expression to match the 'id' string and then check if it is a positive integer.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The middleware function correctly validates the route parameter 'id' before allowing the route to proceed.
